title: AI/ML Intern
bullets: Developed and evaluated supervised ML models on real-world datasets to support data-driven business decisions.
Designed an enhanced Limited Random Forest model, improving predictive accuracy by ~10% and reducing execution time by ~25% via optimized feature selection and constrained tree depth.
Performed EDA to identify patterns, anomalies, and key drivers influencing model performance.
Evaluated models using standard metrics (Accuracy, F1-score) and validated improvements through iterative experimentation.
Translated analytical findings into actionable insights for stakeholders, supporting model adoption.

title: Project Intern
bullets: Assisted in developing web-based applications using ASP.NET and MySQL, focusing on backend logic and database management.
Designed and executed SQL queries to support application workflows and ensure data integrity.
Collaborated with team members to implement and test backend features aligned with project requirements.

title: Applied ML Research Contributor
bullets: Conducted empirical evaluation of ensemble learning techniques, leading to an optimized Limited Random Forest model.
Co-authored a research paper analyzing model efficiency vs accuracy trade-offs (under peer review).
Performed literature review and comparative analysis of ML and cryptographic techniques for secure systems.

title: Volunteer Operations Assistant
bullets: Managed distribution records and inventory data for essential grocery, health, and hygiene items.
Ensured accurate tracking and reporting to support efficient distribution for students and community members in need.
Collaborated with team members to streamline operational workflows and improve data consistency.
